notes: The community college statistics course included on this agreement has been approved as a substitute for Statistics 12 which means it may not transfer as an exact UCLA equivalent.

The Political Science major aims to provide students with understanding of basic political processes and institutions as these operate in different national and cultural contexts. It also covers the interactions between nation states, the changing character of the relations between citizens and governments, and the values and criteria by which the quality of political life is judged. Listed below are the lower division preparation courses for the major. THIS MAJOR IS HIGHLY SELECTIVE. Transfer students must be admitted as Pre-Political Science majors in order to major in Political Science. To be considered for this major, you must complete a MINIMUM OF THREE major preparation courses, by the spring before transfer: One course in Introduction to Political Theory, One course in International Relations/World Politics, One course in Introduction to American Government/Politics, One course in Introduction to Comparative Politics, One course in Politics and Strategy, One course in Diversity and Disagreement, and One course in Statistics. All statistics courses used towards meeting the requirements for this major must be articulated with UCLA's Stats 10. View articulation agreements on https://assist.org to see if your college offers an approved stats course. Not all statistics courses are equivalent to UCLA's Stats 10 even though they may be used for IGETC. All courses must be taken for a letter grade. For more information regarding this major, visit https:// polsci.ucla.edu and https://admission.ucla.edu . PLEASE NOTE: the community college courses listed below have been approved to satisfy the preparation requirements for this major at UCLA, but they may not be exact equivalents of the UCLA courses listed.
AP American or Comparative Political Science and Statistics exams DO NOT meet major prep.
